title: ardisia liebmannii subsp. jalapensis
intro: Ardisia liebmannii subsp. jalapensis, also known as Jalapa, is a plant species in the Ardisia genus, native to Mexico and Central America, characterized by its dark green leaves and red berries. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic activities. The health benefits of Jalapa include reducing fever, relieving rheumatic pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Jalapa is traditionally used in herbal preparations like teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other medicinal plants to enhance its therapeutic effects.
